The PR converts config-audit scrub and legacy cron doctor checks into structured detect/repair health checks while carrying a broader stacked structured-doctor repair conversion across session, sandbox, plugin, gateway, completion, and systemd surfaces.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured doctor repair feature/refactor PR, not a bug report. Source inspection shows the intended code paths, but I did not reproduce the real CLI behavior locally.
commands.review_followup: This protected, draft, stacked PR needs maintainer stack management and real behavior proof rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: Doctor: structure config audit and cron repairs
target.body:
## Summary

Stacked on #84366.

This converts the next two doctor health chunks to real structured detect/repair checks:

- `doctor:config-audit-scrub` -> `core/doctor/config-audit-scrub`
- `doctor:legacy-cron` -> `core/doctor/legacy-cron-store` plus the already-structured `core/doctor/legacy-whatsapp-crontab`

The conversion keeps normal `openclaw doctor` note behavior while making `openclaw doctor --fix` route through the structured repair contract for the mutating work. Cron model override diagnostics still print in fix mode before the structured cron store repair, and aborted config-audit rewrites no longer report a file effect.

| Check | Detect contract | Repair contract | Dry-run/diff behavior | Detect-after proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/config-audit-scrub` | Dry-runs `scrubConfigAuditLog` and reports scrub-needed audit log entries by audit path. | Rewrites historical argv/execArgv through the existing config-audit redactor. | Reports `would-scrub-config-audit-log`; no concrete diff is emitted for secret-bearing audit content. | Test rewrites an unredacted audit log, then validates no remaining finding. |
| `core/doctor/legacy-cron-store` | Inspects the cron store without mutating it and reports legacy job storage by store path. | Normalizes only the selected cron store finding, including legacy job fields, notify fallback, and dreaming payload shape. | Reports `would-normalize-legacy-cron-store` and leaves the store unchanged. | Test repairs a legacy cron store, then validates no remaining finding. |

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-doctor-cron-audit.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-audit-scrub.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `doctor:config-audit-scrub` and `doctor:legacy-cron` now expose structured detector and repair checks with scoped effects, dry-run preview behavior, and detect-after validation.

Real environment tested: Local WSL source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-doctor-next` using repository wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The focused Vitest command above creates real temporary config-audit and cron-store files, runs structured repair flow, and checks dry-run/no-write and detect-after behavior. The tsgo, oxlint, diff check, and autoreview commands above were also run after the accepted autoreview fixes.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 files / 97 tests. `tsgo` completed with exit 0. `oxlint` reported 0 warnings and 0 errors. `git diff --check` completed with exit 0. Final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Config audit scrub removes an unredacted argv token and leaves no remaining finding after repair. Cron store repair removes legacy `jobId`/`notify` state, writes canonical job shape, and leaves no remaining finding after repair. Dry-run paths report concrete file effects and leave files unchanged.

What was not tested: Broad changed/full CI and Crabbox/Testbox lanes were not run for this narrow stacked source/test change.
